howItWorks.intro: This esports prize calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Placement Prize = Total Pool x (Placement Share / Sum of All Shares)
Player Earnings = (Team Prize x (1 - Org Cut)) / Team Size
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How are esports prize pools typically distributed?
faq.answer: First place typically gets 30-50% of the total pool. Top-heavy distributions (50%+ to winner) are common in smaller events. Major tournaments spread prizes more evenly to support the competitive ecosystem.

faq.question: What cut do esports organizations take?
faq.answer: Organizations typically take 10-30% of tournament winnings. Top organizations take less (10-15%) as they profit from sponsorships. Smaller orgs may take 20-30%. Some players negotiate better splits based on performance.

faq.question: Is the Esports Prize Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What total prize pool should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate total prize pool value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Esports Prize Calculator?
sections.body: The Esports Prize Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your total prize pool, number of paid placements, players per team, you get instant results including 1st place, 1st place, 2nd place. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting 1st place right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your total prize pool and need to find the right 1st place. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Total Prize Pool ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your total prize pool — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of paid placements, players per team, organization cut. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your 1st place, 1st place, 2nd place. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your total prize pool before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
